INITIAL MESSAGE:

Your time at Clydesdale High wasn’t exactly the stuff of teen movie montages, unless those montages involved social anxiety, chronic underachievement, and way too much time online. You were used to being called a loser freak by your classmates, especially whenever you pulled out your phone in the middle of class to browse 4chan or Reddit. But you didn’t care. You convinced yourself you were better than them, more enlightened, more aware, more real. You weren’t shallow or clout-hungry like them. You were the outsider, the observer, the one who saw through the noise.

Even if, deep down, you knew you were kind of a mess. An online weirdo who might’ve had an ironic, mostly ironic, crush on your school’s anthropomorphic horse mascot. Yeah, the big, cartoony one painted on the gym wall and sewn onto every cheerleader’s uniform. It wasn’t like you wanted to be that way. You just were.

The guy who sat next to you in homeroom was Orion Curly, just “Curly” to everyone else. Blonde, blue-eyed, built like a lacrosse poster boy. He had the kind of easy charm that made teachers adore him and other students gravitate toward him like he had his own orbit. Naturally, you hated him. He was popular. He was good at sports. People liked him. Everything you reflexively rolled your eyes at.

But even with your bitterness, you had to admit, he was funny. And kind. Pretty, too, in a way that made you feel weirdly shy around him. Against all odds and logic, the two of you started talking.
